description: Trigger evals for the docs-corpus-audit skill dispatch description. 10 should-trigger + 10 should-NOT-trigger. 60/40 train/val split. Validation split is the ship gate (>=0.5 on should-trigger AND <0.5 on should-not-trigger). The hardest constraint is the boundary with docs-sync: queries scoped to a single PR's docs impact must route to docs-sync, NOT here.

train: audit the entire docs corpus and reground every claim against current code
the docs feel stale everywhere -- do a whole-corpus regrounding pass
we're cutting v0.16 next week, sweep the docs for drift before release
we just reshaped the TOC, find every dead nav link
check every page against code, no matter the PR history
run a full pre-release docs audit
val: quarterly docs drift sweep -- catch the accumulated drift
do a holistic regrounding pass on docs/src/content/docs/
post-major-refactor docs sweep across the whole corpus
nothing in the docs matches the CLI anymore, audit and fix it all

train: PR opened: adds --no-cache flag to apm install -- review docs impact
this PR changes apm.lock.yaml integrity field -- assess docs
review the doc updates in PR #1234
write a guide for the new apm preview command
doc-only PR opened -- editorial review please
val: PR opened: rename apm pack to apm bundle, check docs
add a new section about the registry workflow to guides/registries.md
doc-impact review for the auth resolver refactor
PR opened: docs-only rewrite of getting-started/installation.md

notes: All should-NOT-trigger queries are scoped to a single PR or a single page -- those route to docs-sync (PR scope), to doc-writer (writing a new page), or to a direct edit (typo).
The should-trigger queries are scoped to the WHOLE corpus, not a diff. The word 'audit' alone is not sufficient (audit could mean apm audit CLI); the corpus scope is the load-bearing trigger.
Pre-release and post-IA-reshuffle are the canonical maintainer-ask shapes -- both legitimately whole-corpus.
